task:
ATOM A3a â€” Restore the `cocoder/**`-only audit write-boundary as an ordinary-priority capability (the delicate, FIRST rebuild-finishing atom).

WHY: The `cocoder/**`-only REFUSE-boundary used to live ONLY in the now-deleted executor's P7 apply hook. Ordinary-run commit gates pass `scope` but NEVER pass `auditWriteBoundary`, so an `onboard-existing` run today would commit-and-FLAG product-code edits (ADR-0023 Â§3) instead of REFUSING them â€” violating the user-facing promise stated in `packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md` that the audit only ever writes `cocoder/**`. This atom restores REFUSE-on-out-of-boundary for onboarding runs, driven by ordinary-priority data, without changing ordinary runs.

REUSE, DO NOT REBUILD: The enforcement primitive already exists from the retired executor's P5/P6 work â€” `runCommitGate`'s `auditWriteBoundary` param and the `AuditWriteBoundaryError` it throws BEFORE any commit on an out-of-`cocoder/**` path (search `gate.ts`/`commit-gate.test.ts`). Your job is to make an ORDINARY priority run able to set and thread that param; do not write a new enforcement mechanism.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Parse an optional priority frontmatter field `auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]` (a string[] of globs) in `loadPriority` (the priority loader/types). Absent field => undefined/null (ordinary behavior).
2. Thread the parsed value through `RunInput` (and any run-construction path that builds it) so it is available at every commit site.
3. Pass it as the `auditWriteBoundary` argument to `runCommitGate` at EVERY commit chokepoint that an onboarding run hits â€” the per-agent-step gate (`agent-step.ts` ~line 251) AND the oscar / deb / wrap gates (`runner.ts` ~lines 692/828/1008). When the priority has no boundary, pass nothing (unchanged whole-tree commit). When set, the gate must REFUSE (throw `AuditWriteBoundaryError`, zero commit) any changed path outside the boundary, exactly as the executor apply-commit did.
4. Set the field on `packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md` frontmatter: `auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]`.
5. Rename the now-deferred `cocoder-takeover` label literals to `onboard-existing` in `commit-gate.test.ts` and `read-surfaces.test.ts` (the boundary-label identifiers only â€” bundled rename per the owner map, no separate pass).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (prove each with evidence):
- A fake/onboarding run whose priority carries `auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]` and writes a product-code path (e.g. `packages/core/src/foo.ts`) is REFUSED with `AuditWriteBoundaryError` and commits NOTHING (commits === []). Add/extend a test that asserts this end-to-end through the ordinary commit gate (not the deleted executor path).
- An ordinary priority (NO `auditWriteBoundary` field) is UNCHANGED: whole-tree commit behavior preserved, out-of-lane edits still committed-and-flagged per ADR-0023 Â§3, never refused.
- `loadPriority` round-trips the field (present => parsed array; absent => undefined).

MUST NOT BREAK: ordinary priority runs (the overwhelming majority) must behave exactly as before. Keep the whole-tree-commit default fully intact. Match surrounding style; touch only what this atom needs.

VERIFY COMMANDS (run all, paste output): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm -w typecheck`, `node scripts/check-topology.mjs`.

WRITE SCOPE: `packages/core/**` (priority loader + types, `RunInput`, `agent-step.ts`, `runner.ts`, `gate.ts` wiring if needed, the two named test files), `packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md`. Do NOT touch ordinary-run commit semantics, the New-Primary/Drift skeletons, or scaffold seeding (that is A3b, a separate atom).

Report the exact diff and the four command outputs when done.

ATOM A3b â€” Conditionally seed the `onboard-existing` priority into a scaffolded workspace, for EXISTING repos only.

WHY: Onboarding now reaches a workspace as an ordinary scaffold-seeded priority (run_140 founder decision, ADR-0020 Â§7 amendment). The scaffold must drop `onboard-existing` into the repo's `cocoder/priorities/` so an existing repo's FIRST CoCoder interaction is the audit. But ONLY existing repos (source already outside `cocoder/`) get it â€” a brand-new/empty repo does NOT (New-Primary handles that case later). Today `scaffoldCocoderZone` copies the whole template tree UNCONDITIONALLY, so a plain template drop would wrongly seed empty repos too. This atom adds the conditional.

CONTEXT (read first): `packages/core/src/scaffold/scaffold.ts` (`scaffoldCocoderZone` + `copyTree`, create-only recursive copy of `templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/**`), its test `packages/core/tests/scaffold.test.ts`, the caller `scaffoldWorkspaceGovernance` in `packages/daemon/src/routes.ts:337` (passes only `targetRoot`), and the base priority `packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md` (note: precedent â€” `adhoc-session.md` exists in BOTH the base dir and `templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/` with identical content).

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add the template file `templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md`. Its content MUST be the SAME as the base `packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md` â€” INCLUDING the `auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]` frontmatter (CRITICAL: the seeded priority must carry that field or the A3a trust boundary never applies when the workspace runs it). If a baseâ†”template consistency test exists for priorities, keep them in sync.
2. In `scaffoldCocoderZone`, treat `cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md` as a CONDITIONAL-SEED path: the unconditional `copyTree` must NOT copy it (so empty/new repos never receive it). After the unconditional copy, conditionally copy that one file ONLY when the target is an EXISTING repo, and include it in the returned `created` list when seeded.
3. EXISTING-repo predicate (deterministic, document it in a comment): the target is an existing repo iff `targetRoot` already contains at least one entry OTHER than `cocoder` and `.git` (i.e. source/content lives outside the `cocoder/` zone) at scaffold time. An empty repo, or one containing only `.git` and/or the `cocoder/` zone, is NOT existing â†’ do NOT seed. Detect this INSIDE `scaffoldCocoderZone` (one owner, directly unit-testable); do not push the decision to the caller.
4. Preserve all existing scaffold guarantees: create-only (never overwrite), idempotent (re-running seeds nothing new), and the install-tree refusal.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (prove with tests in `scaffold.test.ts`):
- EXISTING repo: scaffold into a temp dir that already contains a stray source entry (e.g. a `src/app.ts` or `package.json` outside `cocoder/`) â†’ `created` INCLUDES `cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md`, and the file exists on disk with the `auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]` frontmatter.
- EMPTY/new repo: scaffold into an empty temp dir (or one containing only `.git`) â†’ `created` does NOT include `cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md`, and the file is absent on disk; the rest of the `cocoder/` zone (adhoc-session.md, assignments.json, etc.) still scaffolds normally.
- Idempotency: a second scaffold over an existing-repo result creates nothing new (no duplicate seed).

MUST NOT BREAK: ordinary scaffold of the rest of the template tree is unchanged; install-tree refusal still throws; `scaffoldWorkspaceGovernance`/`createWorkspace` still succeeds (update any daemon scaffold test that asserts an exact `created` list, since real workspaces are existing repos and will now also seed onboard-existing.md). Match surrounding style.

VERIFY COMMANDS (run all, paste output): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm -w typecheck`, `node scripts/check-topology.mjs`.

WRITE SCOPE: `packages/core/src/scaffold/**`, `templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md`, `packages/core/tests/scaffold.test.ts`, and any daemon scaffold test under `packages/daemon/tests/` that needs its `created` expectation updated. Do NOT touch the New-Primary/Drift skeletons, the commit gate, or the A3a boundary code.

Report the exact diff and the four command outputs when done.

ATOM A4 â€” One-command runnable proof of the Oscar-driven onboard-existing flow (the final rebuild atom), replacing the retired executor proof.

WHY: The onboarding rebuild (run_140â€“141) replaced the standalone phase-executor with three ordinary-priority capabilities. They must be provable in ONE founder-runnable command, the same way the rest of the system is (see `scripts/proof-direct-spine.mjs` for the canonical shape). The old `scripts/proof-takeover-executor.mjs` proved the now-DELETED executor (it imports symbols removed in run_140) and is retired.

HOUSE STYLE (follow `scripts/proof-direct-spine.mjs` exactly): a proof script does NOT reimplement behavior â€” it RUNS THE REAL TESTS that already assert each invariant and maps each to a rebuild clause, printing a PASS/FAIL table and exiting 0 iff every row passes. Drive the actual vitest suites/tests (via `pnpm --filter ... test` with `-t` name filters or whole files), temp dirs only, NO daemon/live-process/window operations.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Add `scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` â€” `node scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` runs the REAL tests backing the THREE rebuilt invariants and prints a green PASS/FAIL table (exit 0 all-pass, non-zero + named failing row otherwise):
   - INVARIANT 1 â€” Onboarding REFUSES product-code writes: an `onboard-existing`-style priority (carrying `auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]`) that changes a path outside `cocoder/**` is REFUSED with `AuditWriteBoundaryError` and commits NOTHING. Backed by the real tests: `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts` test 'refuses onboard-existing product-code writes before the ordinary atom gate commits' AND `packages/core/tests/commit-gate.test.ts` audit-boundary refusal tests.
   - INVARIANT 2 â€” Ordinary runs are UNCHANGED: a priority WITHOUT `auditWriteBoundary` keeps whole-tree commit; out-of-lane edits are committed-and-FLAGGED (ADR-0023 Â§3), never refused. Backed by: `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts` test 'ordinary priorities keep whole-tree commit behavior and only flag out-of-lane files'.
   - INVARIANT 3 â€” Scaffold seeding is CONDITIONAL: an existing repo (source outside `cocoder/`) is seeded the `onboard-existing` priority; an empty/`.git`-only repo is NOT; create-only/idempotent. Backed by: `packages/core/tests/scaffold.test.ts` seeding tests ('seeds onboard-existing only when target already has source content', 'does not seed onboard-existing into a .git-only new repo', the idempotency test).
   Add a top-of-file comment block in the same explanatory style as `proof-direct-spine.mjs` (what green means; what a red row means â€” a specific named failing test, not vague homework).
2. Delete the retired `scripts/proof-takeover-executor.mjs`.
3. Update any references to the old script (grep the repo: `package.json` scripts, `docs/**`, `README*`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, any proof-index) to point at the new script or drop the dead reference. Do NOT touch product code or the priority `.md` governance files under `packages/personas/base/` â€” only references in docs/build config.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `node scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` exits 0 and prints a PASS/FAIL table with one row per invariant (all PASS).
- The table rows map 1:1 to the three rebuilt invariants above and name the real test(s) each row runs.
- `scripts/proof-takeover-executor.mjs` no longer exists; no dangling references to it remain (grep is clean).
- The script reuses the real tests â€” it does NOT re-implement the runner, commit gate, or scaffold.

MUST NOT BREAK: no product code changes; ordinary suites stay green. Match the style/structure of `scripts/proof-direct-spine.mjs`.

VERIFY COMMANDS (run all, paste output): `node scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` (show the table + exit code), `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm -w typecheck`, `node scripts/check-topology.mjs`, and `grep -rn proof-takeover-executor . --exclude-dir=node_modules --exclude-dir=.git` (must return nothing).

WRITE SCOPE: `scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` (new), delete `scripts/proof-takeover-executor.mjs`, and reference-only edits in `package.json` / `docs/**` / `README*` / `ARCHITECTURE.md` if any reference the old script. Do NOT modify runner/commit-gate/scaffold source or `packages/personas/base/**`.

Report the exact diff and all command outputs when done.

ATOM A4 (re-scoped) â€” One-command runnable proof of the Oscar-driven onboard-existing flow, replacing the retired executor proof. The previous attempt's PROOF SCRIPT was correct; it was rejected ONLY because it also rewrote historical/governance docs. This re-scope keeps the exact same script + deletion and makes ZERO documentation edits.

HARD CONSTRAINT â€” DO NOT EDIT ANY DOCS THIS TIME: Make NO changes to `cocoder/SESSION_LOG.md`, `cocoder/decisions/**`, `cocoder/priorities/**`, `docs/**`, `README*`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, or any other prose/governance/owner-map file. Every existing mention of `proof-takeover-executor.mjs` lives in a HISTORICAL or evidentiary record (past session logs, the run_131 build-progress record, the owner-map's file:line evidence) where the old name is CORRECT and must stay verbatim â€” those are append-only history, not live references. There are NO live/operational references to update (no package.json script entry exists for it). Touch ONLY the two script files below.

WHAT TO BUILD (exactly two file operations):
1. ADD `scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` â€” a one-command founder-runnable proof, in the SAME style/structure as `scripts/proof-direct-spine.mjs` (read it first). It does NOT reimplement the runner/commit-gate/scaffold; it RUNS THE REAL named tests via vitest's JSON reporter and maps each to one of the three rebuilt invariants, printing a PASS/FAIL/MISSING table and exiting 0 iff every invariant row passes (non-zero + the named failing/missing test otherwise). Required behavior:
   - Run these real core suites once: `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/commit-gate.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/scaffold.test.ts` (e.g. `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core exec vitest run <files> --reporter=json --outputFile=<tmp>`), parse the JSON, and look up assertion results BY TEST TITLE.
   - INVARIANT 1 'onboarding refuses product-code writes' â€” backed by titles: 'refuses onboard-existing product-code writes before the ordinary atom gate commits', 'refuses onboard-existing audit commits outside cocoder/** before committing', 'refuses P6 ratification apply-commit when any product path is changed'.
   - INVARIANT 2 'ordinary runs are unchanged' â€” backed by title: 'ordinary priorities keep whole-tree commit behavior and only flag out-of-lane files'.
   - INVARIANT 3 'scaffold seeding is conditional' â€” backed by titles: 'seeds onboard-existing only when target already has source content', 'does not seed onboard-existing into a .git-only new repo', 'is idempotent after seeding onboard-existing into an existing repo'.
   - A row is PASS only if every backing title resolves to status 'passed'; if a title is absent from the report it is MISSING (a red row â€” the proof must NOT silently pass if a test was renamed/removed). Use a temp dir for the JSON output and clean it up. Top-of-file comment block in the `proof-direct-spine.mjs` style (what green means; a red row = a specific named failing/missing test, not vague homework).
2. DELETE `scripts/proof-takeover-executor.mjs` (it imports the executor symbols removed in run_140 and is the retired artifact named by ADR-0026).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `node scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` exits 0 and prints a table with all three invariant rows PASS, each naming its backing test(s).
- The script reuses the real tests (no reimplementation of runner/gate/scaffold).
- `scripts/proof-takeover-executor.mjs` no longer exists.
- `git status` shows ONLY: new `scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` + deleted `scripts/proof-takeover-executor.mjs`. NO other file is modified â€” if any doc/governance file shows as modified, revert it; this atom must not touch them.

VERIFY COMMANDS (run all, paste output): `node scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` (show table + exit code), `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm -w typecheck`, `node scripts/check-topology.mjs`, and `git status --porcelain` (must list only the two script paths).

WRITE SCOPE: ONLY `scripts/proof-onboard-existing.mjs` (new) and the deletion of `scripts/proof-takeover-executor.mjs`. Nothing else.

Report the exact diff and all command outputs when done.
